checkpoints.topic: Project shape, media/plugin state, core effect chain, Shake/stillness discovery

facts.note: Bucketed by source folder path substring, Track 1 only.

data.relinkMethod: Media.ReplaceWith(MediaPool.AddMedia(matchedPath)) -- MediaFile.FilePath setter exists but is not publicly accessible from script code
data.verificationMethod: Fresh VEGAS launch against Untitled.relinked.veg, IsOffline() checked with zero relink code running in that session

data.suppressionAttempted: TPT_szOFXExcludedPlugins preference (pre-existing value: 'BCC10_OFX;BCC11_OFX'); appended full bracketed uniqueIDs and bare GUID -- did NOT suppress the dialog

referencedMissingPlugins.note: identified only by class GUID in the dialog, no friendly name shown

data.claim: Every one of the 276 Track-1 video events has exactly 1 VideoMotion keyframe (no Pan/Crop animation anywhere in the project).

facts.note: This is the key reframing finding: the 'screen pump' is a shake-to-zero, not a shake or zoom spike.

recipeB_impact.interpretation: Camera shake decays to exactly 0 within ~0.25-0.37s of the cut; Z Dist only moves 0.9->1.0 (a nudge, not a zoom punch). One exact reusable preset, not per-event hand tuning, for this recipe.

facts.note: Single-sample only (t1_e0) -- do not generalize yet. 275/276 events have a Velocity envelope.

openQuestions: Correlate the 25 'impact' S_Shake events against markers and Track-2 audio events to identify actual kill timing.

checkpoints.topic: Kill-moment localization; velocity envelope generalized across all 275 events

facts.note: Exact-zero timestamp deltas across all 25 samples, not a statistical approximation.

data.interpretation: Track 2 is almost certainly the gunshot/hit-confirmation SFX layer. Every impact-recipe cut is also a beat-aligned gunshot. Most gunshots (85%) are beat-aligned generally, but only 17% of them are also the site of a new cut carrying the special shake-to-zero treatment -- i.e. within a held clip containing multiple audible hits, only the hit that coincides with an actual edit gets the bespoke visual effect.

facts.note: Corrects/refines the checkpoint-1 single-sample note (0.7x plateau was not representative).

data.interpretation: Median plateau is exactly 0.5x across the full corpus, confirming the classic half-speed slow-motion assumption (the earlier single-sample 0.7x reading was not representative). Plateau depth is statistically indistinguishable between impact and non-impact events (median 0.5 both groups) -- velocity ramping and the Shake-based impact treatment are independent editorial layers, not one coupled 'kill recipe'. Entry/exit speeds both comfortably exceed 1x (medians ~2.87x / ~2.76x), confirming ramps genuinely overshoot rather than settling back to normal speed.

openQuestions: Confirm Track 2 event identity directly against SFX filenames/catalog rather than by timing inference alone.
Precise re-count of the 25 impact events' section distribution.
Audio treatment recipes for Track 2 (143 events) and Track 4 (24 events): gain, pitch, effects chain, fades.
Bus/master/track gain chain verification for the -3dB music level claim.
Track-level S_Shake/S_Flicker parameter detail (animated or static at track scope).
First/middle/final-kill variation within multi-kill sequences.
Visual/render confirmation of representative moments.
Standalone missing-plugin capability report.

checkpoints.topic: Audio treatment recipes for the weapon-SFX and whoosh layers; identified export gaps (native FX params, master bus)

data.weaponLibrarySourceFile: Modern Warfare 2 - All Weapons Showcase (Reloads, Sounds, Animations).mp3
data.originalGameplayAudioNote: Each of the 18 is sourced from a distinct original gameplay clip file, isGrouped=true, grouped with its corresponding video event.
data.interpretation: Track 2 is the SFX/audio-replacement layer. Most hits (125) get the curated weapon-showcase library; a minority (18) retain original in-game audio.

All 125 replacement-weapon events use the identical source sample at the identical offset; per-gun sonic variety is produced entirely by per-event Pitch Shift + Reverb settings, not by selecting different source material. Confirms the hypothesis exactly as posed.

data.reason: These are native VEGAS/VST effects (isOFX=false). The current inspector's deep parameter/keyframe export only covers the OFX parameter family (OFXParameter/OFXKeyframe); native effects only get identity fields (plugin name/uniqueID/classID, bypass, currentPresetName -- which is null here).
data.consequence: Cannot yet quantify how much Pitch Shift/Reverb vary per weapon-SFX event, nor read the Noise Gate/EQ/Compressor settings on any audio track.
data.fix: Needs a follow-up inspector pass into the classic (non-OFX) Effect.Keyframes/automation API -- not yet explored or reflected.

data.interpretation: The song track has zero envelopes at track or event level -- attenuation is a flat -3dB with no ducking/automation around gunfire.

data.reason: Project.MasterBus (and VideoBus) were never read by the inspector.
data.consequence: The -3dB figures above are track-fader-only; true final perceived attenuation (Codex's explicit caution) cannot be confirmed until the master bus chain is captured.
data.fix: Add Project.MasterBus / Project.VideoBus export (both are AudioBusTrack/VideoBusTrack instances, same Effects/Envelopes shape already handled by the existing ExportEffects/ExportEnvelopes functions -- straightforward to add).

openQuestions: Read native (non-OFX) effect parameters for Pitch Shift/Reverb (weapon SFX) and Track EQ/Compressor/Noise Gate (all audio tracks).
Export Project.MasterBus / VideoBus to close the gain-chain verification.
Cross-reference the 24 whoosh events against transitions/section boundaries.
Closer look at the one tightly-spaced impact pair (t1_e272/t1_e274) as the best multi-kill candidate.
Visual/render confirmation of representative moments.
Standalone missing-plugin capability report.

checkpoints.topic: Multi-kill clustering (negative result) and track-level Shake/Flicker static baseline

facts.note: Corrects an assumption -- this argues against a tightly-clustered multi-kill pattern in this specific recipe.

data.interpretation: 25 impact events read as evenly-spaced highlight beats (mostly 6-14s apart), not tightly-clustered multi-kill bursts. The 'first/middle/final kill in a multi-kill' question has little to grab onto in this recipe's data specifically; the one exception (closer-section pair, 2.09s apart) is the best candidate for an actual back-to-back pair and merits a render check.

The whole-track ambient layer is a deliberately subtle, constant 'handheld/damaged-film' texture (low shake amplitude, slow shake frequency, fast fine-grained flicker), consistent with the Track-0 vintage-film-damage overlay theme. Because the baseline is already subtle, the per-event Amplitude dip to exactly 0 at impact cuts (checkpoint 1, Section 3) reads as a bigger relative contrast -- 'quietly textured' to 'perfectly still' right at the payoff.

openQuestions: Read native (non-OFX) effect parameters for Pitch Shift/Reverb (weapon SFX) and Track EQ/Compressor/Noise Gate (all audio tracks).
Export Project.MasterBus / VideoBus to close the gain-chain verification.
Cross-reference the 24 whoosh events against transitions/section boundaries.
Closer look at the one tightly-spaced impact pair (t1_e272/t1_e274) as the best multi-kill candidate.
Visual/render confirmation of representative moments.
Standalone missing-plugin capability report.

checkpoints.topic: Gain chain fully closed (MasterBus/VideoBus empty); classic-effect automation confirmed absent everywhere (hard capability limit, not an open question anymore)

data.interpretation: No hidden bus/master gain adjustment or effect exists anywhere in the project. Combined with checkpoint 3 (song track/event has zero envelopes) and the track-fader values already recorded, the full gain chain is now confirmed: -3.0dB song / -3.6dB SFX is exactly what reaches the render, with no automation or bus modification at any stage.

data.reflectionConfirmedApiLimit: Keyframe.Preset has no getter (write-only); CurrentPresetName returns null for these effects.
data.conclusion: This is now a confirmed, permanent capability limit, not an open question: the exact per-event Pitch Shift/Reverb/EQ/Compressor settings are not readable through ScriptPortal.Vegas at all. The mechanism (one source sample, per-event Pitch Shift + Reverb, statically configured, no timeline animation) is proven; the exact numeric values are not accessible without binary-format parsing, which is explicitly out of scope.

openQuestions: Cross-reference the 24 whoosh events against transitions/section boundaries.
Closer look at the one tightly-spaced impact pair (t1_e272/t1_e274) as the best multi-kill candidate.
Visual/render confirmation of representative moments.

Consolidate into the full required deliverable set.

checkpoints.topic: Whoosh-to-cinematic transition timing; closest analog to a multi-kill sequence

data.note: Frequent middle<->cinematic weaving from ~70s onward, roughly every 5-6s once established.

outliers.note: before the middle<->cinematic pattern is fully established
leads into the closer section -- longer, deliberate final buildup

data.interpretation: Whoosh is a lead-in transition cue specifically for cuts into cinematic cutaways, consistently anticipating the cut by ~2-3.5s rather than landing on it.
Fade-out spans the entire event -- the whole clip is one continuous decay from an instant onset, not a gated stinger.

Best available example of intentional back-to-back impact structuring in the whole project. Per checkpoint 4's negative result, this reads as a closer-specific punctuation device, not evidence of a general first/middle/final-kill recipe elsewhere in the edit.

openQuestions: Visual/render confirmation of representative moments.
Standalone missing-plugin capability report.
Consolidate into the full required deliverable set.

checkpoints.topic: Visual confirmation via 16 SaveSnapshot frame captures; CORRECTS checkpoint 1's Section 3.1 characterization of the impact mechanism

ordinaryRecipeSample.observation: all three frames sharp, no visible blur or distortion

impactRecipeSample.observation: pre=sharp, at(+53ms)=strong directional motion-blur smear with chromatic fringing, settled(+0.6s)=sharp again with game's own kill-confirmation text visible

data.correctedInterpretation: The impact recipe is not merely 'shake amplitude decays to zero'. It stacks a SECOND S_Shake instance with the Motion Blur boolean explicitly forced true (a static, non-animated override) at Mo Blur Length 0.8 and 2.0 on the two instances respectively -- identical across every sampled event. Because Sapphire's shake motion-blur length scales with actual per-frame shake motion, the brief above-baseline Amplitude spike (1.1->0.7) at the cut produces a genuine visible motion-blur smear, and killing Amplitude to 0 within ~0.25-0.37s kills the blur too, producing a sharp landed frame. This was only discovered by rendering actual frames -- parameter values alone (checkpoint 1) suggested a much simpler 'shake to zero = stillness' story that undersold what's actually on screen.

data.observation: completely blank canvas, confirming no content exists before the first video event at ~26.6s
genuinely desaturated/grayscale, confirming the project's one-off Black and White effect renders as expected
Both frames (at +47ms and +297ms into the 834ms-long t1_e274) still show heavy blur, consistent with the effect's ~250-370ms decay window overlapping most of this short event's visible duration.

openQuestions: Consolidate into the full required deliverable set.
Optional: audio-synced short segment renders if a suitable RenderTemplate can be found/built.

checkpoints.topic: Both recipes confirmed as exact byte-identical presets; copy-paste-trim workflow inferred from fixed-duration keyframe timing

data.conclusion: Directly and completely answers 'one preset or many variations' (Codex Q4) for both clusters: both the 224-event and 25-event groups are single reusable presets, not per-event hand tuning, across every parameter checked.

data.observation: Every sampled event carries the identical 1.101s-long curve regardless of its own (often shorter) length -- the curve's tail is simply never reached on short events.
data.interpretation: Inferred-High: reveals the actual editorial workflow -- one fully-configured template event's effect chain was copy-pasted onto every subsequent cut, then each was trimmed to its own final length independently, without re-timing the keyframes. Reusable insight for an automated planner: anchor the recipe to event start and let natural trimming truncate it, rather than rescaling the curve per clip duration.

openQuestions: Final consolidation into the full required deliverable set (in progress).

checkpoints.topic: Compositing modes and crossfade transitions (closes Codex Priority 9)

data.interpretation: Screen-mode blending is the conventional technique for layering a dust/scratch/light-leak texture over footage without darkening it -- confirms the overlay's intended visual role.

data.conclusion: Overlap duration exactly equals both the outgoing event's fadeOut length and the incoming event's fadeIn length in every sampled case (to 6 decimal places) -- this is VEGAS's default auto-crossfade from overlapping fade curves, NOT a dedicated Transition plug-in object. Distinct from the 3 explicit uses of the missing Boris FX Vector Blur Dissolve transition. ~41% of cuts use a short (17-367ms, median 50ms) built-in crossfade; the remaining 59% are true hard cuts; zero timeline gaps anywhere.

checkpoints.topic: Self-audit prompted by 'are you certain every angle is covered' -- closes several previously-unexamined schema fields, finds one genuinely missed technique (reversed subclip), and one remaining unconfirmed gap (CustomData)

data.mediaFilePath: Call of Duty  Modern Warfare 2 (2022) 2022-10-28 - 15-26-10-11-DVR_1.mp4 - subclip 1 (reversed)
data.interpretation: Not previously reported in any prior checkpoint or in inferred-effect-presets.json. A distinct one-off technique: a reversed-playback subclip created from cinematic footage. Should get its own preset entry (accent.reversed-cinematic-clip) if reproduced.
Closes a real potential blind spot in the earlier corpus-wide animated-parameter scan, which only fully implemented Double/Integer/Boolean/Choice types.

No evidence of deliberately-unsynchronized markers -- ordinary cuts are loosely beat-adjacent (1-5 frames), only impact cuts are frame-exact (<1ms). Refines rather than contradicts the earlier finding.

data.reason: CustomDataContainer field does not exist in any inspector schema version (v1-v3).
data.consequence: Genuinely unverified, not confirmed-empty like the items above. Low expected yield for this project type but not checked.

checkpoints.status: All Codex priorities and original task-spec requirements addressed, plus a self-audit pass. Remaining known gaps (native effect exact values, CustomData, render-template export, independent timecode cross-check) are explicitly documented, not silently absent. See inferred-editing-strategies.md Section 15 for the final deliverable list.

checkpoints.topic: Adversarial verification pass -- actively tried to falsify prior headline conclusions, reconciled with Codex's independent reports, delivered 12 new files. Full reconciliation ledger: reconciled-final-findings.json.

data.method: source-run grouping (46 contiguous same-source-file runs across all 276 Track-1 events)
data.pattern: long run of ordinary-family events, escalating to impact-family treatment at or immediately before the run's last event
data.priorClaim: no clustering pattern exists among the 25 impact events
data.whyPriorClaimWasWrong: checked spacing only within the impact-event population itself, not the ordinary events surrounding them in the same source run
data.crossValidatedAgainst: Codex representative-moments-validation.md (t1_e51->t1_e55->t1_e60 example), found via a different method

data.method: SHA1 signature over full effect chain + all keyframe times/values/interpolation, all 276 Track-1 events
data.priorClaim: both recipes are single byte-identical presets with zero variation across all sampled events
data.correction: each recipe is one reusable template applied at more than one fixed duration, not one literal invariant preset

data.crossfadeMechanism: VEGAS automatic crossfade generation (114/114 Smooth/Smooth curves, 112/114 exact-symmetric fade lengths matching overlap duration) -- not hand-authored transition curves

data.method: render ablation, 2 events x 4 track/event FX bypass combinations, PNG pixel metrics
data.finding: event-level FX dominates/masks track-level ambient FX on impact events; track-level FX proportionally more visible on ordinary events

data.markerGridSpacing: half-time (every 2nd beat), k=2 dominant at 115/153 (~75%) of intervals
data.globalRigidGridTest: FAILED -- local residual 71ms (13.6% of beat) but global phase-fit residual 229ms; windowed local-BPM 96-150 range within stable ~113-122 BPM window means
data.implication: song is not on one fixed extrapolatable tempo grid across its 240s runtime; any automated beat-grid cut-placement must track locally, not extrapolate

data.pairingRule: 100% (18/18) of gameplay-audio events start at 0ms delta from, and share source file with, a 'none'-family Track-1 video event

data.trackLevelEnvelopes: 0 across all 5 tracks (extending already-confirmed 0 at bus/master level)
data.conclusion: no ducking/volume-automation mechanism exists anywhere in the project; any perceived duck during gunfire is psychoacoustic masking only, not authored

data.method: controlled on/off ablation on t1_e3 (BlurMoCurves + Glow bypassed vs active), identical timestamp
data.conclusion: real, measurable, not negligible -- narrows but does not retract 'reads as a plain cut at normal viewing speed'

data.searched: all Track-level and Event-level Effects entries across all 5 tracks and all events, project-inspection-v3.json
data.conclusion: confirms by elimination (not direct proof) that the 4 missing plugins are not attached via Track/Event Effects; VEGAS's Transition object was never exported by any inspector version, leaving the original 'used as transition' classification unconfirmed structurally

data.finalOutputProcessing: none found -- no adjustment track, no master-bus effect, no render-time LUT

data.rootCause: script compile error (TrackEvent.Effects vs required VideoEvent cast), not a novel dialog type
data.contributingFactor: title-only window matching (FindWindow by title alone, not scoped to launched PID) plausibly prevented auto-dismissal when a redundant concurrent VEGAS instance was present
data.safetyBehaviorConfirmed: automation did not blind-click the unidentified dialog; killed the stuck process instead
data.recommendedFix: PID-scoped window matching + refuse-to-launch-if-any-VEGAS-instance-exists, per docs/vegas-integration-probe.md's already-prescribed design

openQuestions: OFX keyframe time-domain reference frame (event-relative vs timeline-absolute) -- Part 3 fixture testing not attempted this pass
Exact chain location of the 4 missing plugins (Transition object never exported by any inspector version)
GPU vs CPU render pixel differences -- not tested
Exact per-event Pitch Shift / Reverb parameter values on the 125 weapon-SFX events -- confirmed permanently unreadable via classic-effect API limits
checkpoints.status: Adversarial verification pass complete. See adversarial-verification-report.md (narrative) and reconciled-final-findings.json (full Confirmed/Revised/Falsified/StillUnresolved ledger) for the complete synthesis.
